source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A Hall probe with dimensions as shown in Fig. 2.81 has conductivity $\sigma$ and carries charge density $\rho$. The probe is placed in an unknown magnetic field $B$ oriented along the $+y$ direction. An external potential $V_{\text {ext }}$ is applied to two ends producing an electric field in the $+z$ direction. Derive an expression for $B$ in terms of $V_{\text {Hall }}, V_{\text {ext }}, \sigma, \rho$ and the dimensions of the probe.
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string, not a file path or URL

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object string instead of a file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干明确写明“dimensions as shown in Fig. 2.81”，尺寸信息来自图而非纯文本。
requires_image=true 且 text_dominant=false，说明解题主要依赖图像而不是文字。
需要利用探针几何尺寸 h、l 等建立电压与电场关系，具有明显空间/示意图依赖。
multi_step: 题目要求“derive an expression for B”，不是直接读图，需要把 Hall 电压、外加电压、导电率、载流子密度和尺寸联合起来推导。
至少涉及多步物理关系组合：外加电场与电流/电流密度关系、Hall 平衡关系、几何尺寸与电压换算。
答案是符号表达式而非单个数值，通常意味着需要连续代入和化简。
verifiability: answer_type=short_text，目标答案是明确的闭式公式，输出形式清晰。
给定参考答案 $B=\frac{V_{\text{Hall}}}{V_{\text{ext}}}\cdot\frac{\rho l}{\sigma h}$，可做符号等价核验。
registry_passed=true，且题目与答案文本均存在，便于后续规则或人工校验。

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A Hall probe with dimensions as shown in Fig. 2.81 has conductivity $\sigma$ and carries charge density $\rho$. The probe is placed in an unknown magnetic field $B$ oriented along the $+y$ direction. An external potential $V_{\text {ext }}$ is applied to two ends producing an electric field in the $+z$ direction. Derive an expression for $B$ in terms of $V_{\text {Hall }}, V_{\text {ext }}, \sigma, \rho$ and the dimensions of the probe.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string, not a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: For equilibrium we have $qE_{\text {Hall }}=qBv$. As $E_{\text {Hall }}=\frac{V_{\text {Hall }}}{h}$ and $v=\frac{j}{\rho}=\frac{\sigma E_{\text {ext }}}{\rho}=\frac{\sigma}{\rho}\cdot\frac{V_{\text {ext }}}{l}$, the above gives $\frac{V_{\text {Hall }}}{h}=B\frac{\sigma}{\rho}\cdot\frac{V_{\text {ext }}}{l}$, or $B=\frac{V_{\text {Hall }}}{V_{\text {ext }}}\cdot\frac{\rho l}{\sigma h}$.
